components.role: Runs the reasoning loop, tools, permissions, hooks, MCP client, and task lifecycle.
components.failure_mode: Context Continuum marks the task noncompliant or unavailable; it does not replace the Codex loop.

components.role: Stores and applies ChatGPT or official OpenAI API authentication.
inputs: operator login or API-key entry through Codex
components.failure_mode: The lane is unavailable and doctor fails closed; cctx never receives or persists the credential.

components.role: Applies and reverses only product-owned binary, plugin, hook, MCP, catalog, and config state.
components.failure_mode: Abort and preserve prior bytes; refuse rollback over later user edits.

components.role: Generates the version-pinned one-model Sol catalog and calibrated client policy.
components.failure_mode: Unknown schema, drift, or non-Sol content fails closed without modifying active config.

components.role: Validates documented hook envelopes and converts them to canonical events.
components.failure_mode: Malformed or oversized input is rejected or quarantined without emitting invalid hook protocol.

components.role: Provides the sole durable context authority using SQLite WAL, FTS5, hashes, and checkpoints.
components.failure_mode: Transaction rollback and integrity failure; compaction and rollover remain blocked until verification succeeds.

components.role: Selects bounded, provenance-rich historical data without granting it instruction authority.
components.failure_mode: Return an empty capsule or structured error; never broaden scope or omit the untrusted-data boundary.

components.role: Checkpoints and blocks automatic or manual compaction, then directs verified rollover.
components.failure_mode: Halt the active turn and preserve evidence; never allow compaction after a partial or failed checkpoint.

components.role: Carries a verified recovery capsule into a fresh compliant Codex task before the input ceiling.
components.failure_mode: Leave the source task preserved and stopped; do not invent a replacement reasoning loop.

components.role: Exposes bounded store, search, fetch, pin, checkpoint, export, and forget operations.
components.failure_mode: Deny invalid, oversized, or out-of-scope requests without changing model/config authority.

components.role: Produces the authoritative compliance verdict from model, auth, catalog, config, guard, and store evidence.
components.failure_mode: Unknown, stale, ambiguous, or unproven state is noncompliant and blocks ordinary work.

trust_transitions.data: install, update, rollback, or uninstall intent and paths
trust_transitions.control: explicit command, dry run, canonical paths, ownership manifest

trust_transitions.data: owned config, plugin, hooks, MCP declaration, and catalog path
trust_transitions.control: atomic write, exact backup, conflict detection, rollback
trust_transitions.failure: restore prior bytes or fail closed

trust_transitions.data: ChatGPT login or official OpenAI API credential
trust_transitions.control: Codex-owned authentication flow; credential excluded from cctx

trust_transitions.data: opaque authenticated provider session
trust_transitions.control: Codex authentication boundary
trust_transitions.failure: request fails and doctor remains noncompliant

trust_transitions.data: structured hook envelope containing operator, repository, tool, and model text
trust_transitions.control: schema, size, event-type, and timeout validation
trust_transitions.failure: reject, quarantine, or emit a visible bounded error

trust_transitions.data: canonical event with provenance and hash
trust_transitions.control: transaction, size ceiling, idempotency, monotonic sequence

trust_transitions.data: PreCompact or PostCompact lifecycle event
trust_transitions.control: event validation and mandatory policy dispatch
trust_transitions.failure: block PreCompact or mark PostCompact as an incident

trust_transitions.data: checkpoint barrier and transcript snapshot request
trust_transitions.control: WAL checkpoint, fsync policy, hash manifest

trust_transitions.data: verified checkpoint manifest
trust_transitions.control: independent record-count and hash verification

trust_transitions.data: continue=false hook response and recovery guidance
trust_transitions.control: valid bounded hook protocol and repeated-trigger suppression
trust_transitions.failure: visible stopped turn, never silent continuation

trust_transitions.data: model- or operator-originated MCP request
trust_transitions.control: tool schema, authorization scope, pagination, timeout, size limits

trust_transitions.data: validated retrieval intent and budget
trust_transitions.control: allowlisted operation and store namespace

trust_transitions.data: parameterized exact or ranked query
trust_transitions.control: prepared statements, hard result/token limits, canonical scope

trust_transitions.data: historical records and provenance
trust_transitions.control: hash verification and untrusted-content classification

trust_transitions.data: bounded untrusted recall capsule
trust_transitions.control: delimiters, source IDs, hashes, token and byte ceilings
trust_transitions.failure: truncate by contract or return an error

trust_transitions.data: structured MCP result containing untrusted historical text
trust_transitions.control: provenance labels and no instruction-authority elevation
trust_transitions.failure: Codex receives a visible structured error

trust_transitions.data: Sol-only catalog, compatibility verdict, and hash manifest
trust_transitions.control: deterministic generation, one-model invariant, schema/version guard

trust_transitions.data: verified checkpoint and bounded recovery capsule
trust_transitions.control: source IDs, hashes, task namespace, freshness check

trust_transitions.data: fresh-task launch request with exact Sol policy and recovery capsule
trust_transitions.control: doctor preflight and successor identity verification

trust_transitions.data: pin, export, checkpoint, or forget request
trust_transitions.control: explicit scope, dry run for deletion, audit record
